name: Castile and León (Spain)

colors.symbolism: the gules field of Castile, on which the gold castle is charged
the silver (argent) field of Leon, on which the purple lion is charged
the gold of the three-towered castle of Castile

description: A quartered field reproducing the arms of Castile and Leon: red quarters in the upper hoist and lower fly each bearing a gold three-towered castle, and white quarters in the upper fly and lower hoist each bearing a purple lion rampant crowned.
history: Codified in Article 4 of the Statute of Autonomy of 25 February 1983. The design transposes the medieval quartered arms of the Crown of Castile-Leon (granted by Alfonso X the Wise in the 13th century after the union of the two kingdoms) directly to a flag.

features.symbolism: The quartered design directly reproduces the historic arms of the Crown of Castile and Leon: castles of Castile alternating with lions of Leon.

features.role: three-towered castle of Castile, charged on each red quarter (upper-hoist and lower-fly)
features.symbolism: The gold castle on red is the canting arms of the Kingdom of Castile (castillo = castle), in use since the 12th century.

features.role: lion rampant crowned of Leon, charged on each white quarter (upper-fly and lower-hoist)
features.symbolism: The purple lion on silver is the canting arms of the Kingdom of Leon (leon = lion), in use since the late 12th century.
